quality_assessment: Good quality with minor improvements needed

all_violations.issue: No cleanup in test.afterAll() - API tests create data but never clean up
all_violations.recommendation: Add test.afterAll() to delete any equipos created during tests to prevent state pollution

all_violations.issue: createdData object defined but never populated - creates confusion about test data lifecycle
all_violations.recommendation: Either populate createdData with seeded equipment IDs or remove the unused tracking object

all_violations.issue: No explicit cleanup - tests may leave selected equipment in UI state
all_violations.recommendation: Add test.afterEach() to clear form state and selection between tests

all_violations.issue: Manual authentication logic with complex cookie parsing - should use authenticatedAPICall helper
all_violations.recommendation: Replace manual auth with authenticatedAPICall() helper from ../../helpers/auth.helpers for consistency and maintainability

all_violations.issue: Request listener pattern for tracking API calls - could be race-prone if not properly awaited
all_violations.recommendation: Consider using page.route() to intercept and count requests more deterministically

all_violations.issue: createdData tracking exists but is not populated - cleanup loop will not execute
all_violations.recommendation: Populate createdData.equipos array when seeding test data, or remove the tracking

all_violations.issue: createUserWithCapabilities imported but never used in tests
all_violations.recommendation: Either use the factory to seed test users or remove the unused import

all_violations.issue: No helper imports - should use authenticatedAPICall and factories for consistency
all_violations.recommendation: Import authenticatedAPICall and createUserWithCapabilities to match P0 test patterns

all_violations.issue: Manual token extraction in test.beforeAll() adds overhead - could use storageState for faster auth
all_violations.recommendation: Use storageState: 'playwright/.auth/admin.json' for API tests to eliminate login overhead

high_severity_violations.issue: No cleanup in test.afterAll() - API tests create data but never clean up
high_severity_violations.recommendation: Add test.afterAll() to delete any equipos created during tests to prevent state pollution

top_recommendations.recommendation: Add test.afterAll() to API tests for cleanup - delete any equipos created during tests to prevent state pollution in parallel runs
Add test.afterEach() to P1 E2E tests to clear form state and equipment selection between tests
Replace manual authentication logic in API tests with authenticatedAPICall() helper for consistency
Either populate createdData with seeded equipment IDs or remove the unused tracking object in P0 tests
Use storageState for API tests instead of manual token extraction to eliminate login overhead
Remove unused import createUserWithCapabilities or use it to seed test users in P0 tests
Import authenticatedAPICall and factories in P1 tests to match P0 test patterns for consistency

strengths_summary: 100% test ID coverage - every test has traceable ID (P0-E2E-001, P0-API-001, P1-E2E-001, etc.)
100% priority marker coverage - all tests clearly marked P0 or P1
100% BDD format - all tests have Given-When-Then in JSDoc comments
No hard waits detected - all use waitForResponse() for deterministic timing
No if/else control flow in test bodies - all tests execute same path every time
Network-first pattern applied correctly in all E2E tests
Tests use storageState for auth - no login state pollution
All tests use test.skip() in RED phase - no wasted execution during development
